wrong_answers.question: A 72-year-old male (weight 68 kg, height 5'7") with CrCl of 42 mL/min is receiving high-dose methotrexate (12 g/m²) for osteosarcoma. At 48 hours post-infusion, his methotrexate level is 1.8 µmol/L. Which of the following is the most appropriate intervention?
wrong_answers.your_answer: Administer glucarpidase (carboxypeptidase G2) immediately without waiting for leucovorin
wrong_answers.correct_answer: Increase leucovorin to 100 mg/m² IV every 3 hours and ensure aggressive urine alkalinization
